title: What is data scientist - HW1
text:
I think being a data scientist is about using data to answer meaningful questions and support other department for decision-making. Based on the class material and the working experience, I think the following parts is really critical for being a data scientist: - The research question should be clear. - Data cleaning and management should be careful. - Method of analysis should be chosen after detailed consideration. - Interpreting the result in proper language. - The result should be communicated to others clearly and accurately.



I think data scientists and statisticians have many similarities. They both work with data, and quantitative methods are used. They both target on getting a proper, accurate conclusion on the research question. For the difference, I think statisticians focus more on the model, inference, and study design, but data scientists focus more on programming and data management.



I think I’m some position between data scientist and statisticians. As a data analyst in maternal and child health research, I work on proposal analysis, but I also responsible for the data management and data harmonization at work.





y <- density(iris$Petal.Width)





[1] "density"







[1] "list"







List of 7
 $ x        : num [1:512] -0.656 -0.648 -0.64 -0.633 -0.625 ...
 $ y        : num [1:512] 0.00163 0.0018 0.00199 0.00219 0.00241 ...
 $ bw       : num 0.252
 $ n        : int 150
 $ call     : language density.default(x = iris$Petal.Width)
 $ data.name: chr "iris$Petal.Width"
 $ has.na   : logi FALSE
 - attr(*, "class")= chr "density"
